title: Chain Split Risk
description: Chain split risk is the ecosystem cost created when a contentious protocol upgrade leaves incompatible rule sets active at the same time. Persistent splits can impose replay, reorg, infrastructure, and coordination costs even when each side keeps producing blocks.

resources.title: Bitcoin Dev Philosophy: Costs of a Split
resources.notes: Direct conceptual framing for why incompatible upgrade outcomes impose lasting costs on users and infrastructure.

resources.title: BTC Transcripts: Socialized Costs Of Hard Forks
resources.notes: Concrete examples of replay, privacy, reorg, and coordination costs created by contentious splits.

resources.title: The Blocksize War
resources.notes: Best long-form case study for how split risk and activation conflict spill across wallets, exchanges, miners, and users.
